items.label: Development Complete — Schemas
messages: Now let me explore the existing schemas package structure and check what's already there.
Now I have enough context. Let me check the tsconfig and then start implementing.
Good. Now I have all the context I need. Let me create the task schemas and update the barrel export.
Now add tests for the task schemas, following the existing test patterns:
Now append the task schema tests at the end of the test file:
Now let me build and test:
All 66 tests pass (30 existing + 36 new). Build and tests both pass with zero errors. Now let me run post-change review:
Review shows no breaking changes and minimal structural impact. The new `tasks.ts` file isn't showing yet because it's a new (untracked) file. Let me now commit and complete:
Now mark the pipeline phase as complete:

Schema development is complete. Here's a summary of what was delivered:  ## Summary  ### Created: `packages/schemas/src/tasks.ts` - **`TaskStatusSchema`** — `z.enum(["TODO", "IN_PROGRESS", "DONE"])` for Kanban column states - **`TaskSchema`** — Full task entity with `id` (UUID), `workspaceId`, `title` (1-200 chars), `status`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt` (ISO-8601 datetime) - **`CreateTaskSchema`** — POST request body with `title` (1-200 chars); `workspaceId` injected server-side - **`UpdateTaskStatusSchema`** — PATCH request body with `status` (TaskStatusSchema) - All schemas export inferred TypeScript types via `z.infer<>`  ### Modified: `packages/schemas/src/index.ts` - Added barrel exports for all 4 schemas and 4 types from `./tasks.js`  ### Modified: `packages/schemas/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts` - Added **36 new tests** covering all task schemas with valid inputs, invalid inputs, boundary values (200-char title), missing fields, extra field stripping, and error messages - **All 66 tests pass** (30 existing + 36 new)  ### Quality Gates - ✅ `npm run build` — zero errors - ✅ `npx jest --verbose` — 66/66 tests passing - ✅ `roam_review_change` — no breaking changes, minimal structural impact - ✅ Zod v3 (`^3.24.0`) as specified

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh backend "feat(schemas): add TaskStatus, Task, CreateTask, UpdateTaskStatus Zod schemas for kanban board" 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app && npm run pipeline:complete kanban-task-board-enhanced schema-dev 2>&1
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && bash tools/autonomous-factory/agent-commit.sh pipeline "chore(pipeline): mark Development Complete — Schemas" 2>&1

items.label: Infrastructure Written — Terraform
messages: Now I have a clear picture. The `cosmos.tf` file from `feature/webhook-dispatcher` hasn't been merged yet, so I need to create it with the prerequisite resources plus the new Tasks container. Let me implement all the infra changes.  **Intent: Creating cosmos.tf with prerequisite resources + Tasks container since webhook-dispatcher branch not yet merged.**
Now update the OpenAPI spec with the task endpoints:
Now update the validate-infra.sh hook with Cosmos DB reachability check:
Now let me run the Terraform validation gate:
Terraform validate passes. Now let's run the plan:

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git branch -a | grep -i webhook 2>/dev/null; git log --oneline -20 2>/dev/null | head -20

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git show feature/webhook-dispatcher:apps/sample-app/infra/outputs.tf 2>/dev/null | grep -A5 cosmos; echo "---"; git show feature/webhook-dispatcher:apps/sample-app/infra/mai
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git log --oneline main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | head -5; echo "---"; git branch 2>/dev/null | head -10
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra && az account show > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "Azure CLI authenticated" || echo "Not authenticated"
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/sample-app/infra && terraform plan -var-file=dev.tfvars -out=tfplan -no-color 2>&1 | tee plan-output.txt | tail -40
